output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Get It Back in 60 Seconds
output.meta_description: Landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights and send a state-specific demand letter in 60 seconds at Deposits-Back.com.
content_outline: The Real Reason Landlords Withhold Security Deposits
What Landlords Can (and Cannot) Legally Deduct
State-by-State Deadlines: How Long Is Too Long?
The 60-Second Letter of Demand That Actually Works
When to Escalate to Small Claims Court
key_points: Landlords in most states must return security deposits within 14 to 60 days depending on jurisdiction.
Normal wear and tear, pre-existing damage, and routine cleaning cannot be legally deducted from your deposit.
A formal letter of demand citing state law is the fastest, most effective first step before court.
Deposits-Back.com generates a legally sound demand letter in 60 seconds for a one-time $19.99 fee with no signup or subscription.
output.cta: Don't let your landlord keep what's yours. Create your deposit return demand letter in 60 seconds for just $19.99—no signup, no subscription. Go to Deposits-Back.com now.
output.target_audience: Problem-aware US renters whose landlord is withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposit and who need a fast, affordable legal tool to recover their money.
social_hooks: POV: You moved out 30 days ago and your landlord just ghosted your $2,000 deposit...
3 things your landlord legally CANNOT deduct from your security deposit.
My landlord tried to keep my entire deposit. I got it back in 5 days with one letter. No lawyer. No subscription.
If your landlord hasn't returned your deposit within 30 days, they might already owe you double.
Stop letting your landlord 'process' your deposit for months. 60 seconds. $19.99. Done.

short_form_scripts.body: I used a formal letter of demand from Deposits-Back.com. It cites state law, sets a hard deadline, and puts legal pressure on your landlord instantly. Most landlords fold immediately because they know they're in the wrong.
short_form_scripts.cta: Link in bio. 60 seconds, $19.99, no signup. Stop letting them steal your money.

short_form_scripts.hook: 3 deductions your landlord is NOT allowed to make from your deposit.
short_form_scripts.body: One: Normal wear and tear. Two: Pre-existing damage. Three: Routine cleaning. If you see these on your itemized list, that's illegal. You need to dispute it immediately with a formal demand letter.
short_form_scripts.cta: Generate yours at Deposits-Back.com in 60 seconds. Flat fee, no subscription.

short_form_scripts.hook: If your landlord hasn't returned your deposit within 30 days, watch this.
short_form_scripts.body: Every state has a legal deadline. Miss it, and your landlord could owe you penalties, sometimes double or triple your deposit. But you have to demand it officially first.
